actor.description: Full-stack Developer @ Volvo Group | Cloud-Native (Kubernetes, Microservices) | Cloud Architect | DevOps | FinOps

data.commentary:
As we embark on our journey under #Hackday2026, we start as strangers, having just met in the meeting room this Monday. While no one knows if we are destined to win, I hold that belief firmly. When team members approached me one by one to join my team, my response was simple: yes, why not.

Key aspects to consider include:
1. As the team leader, ambition to win is crucial.
2. Fostering collaboration among team members and earning their trust in a short time is essential.

I recognize that no one expects our team to win except for me. Yet, they trust and support me to the best of their abilities. This gives us the opportunity to validate our proposal in the business world. I am dedicated to ensuring they leave with something meaningful to share, rather than disappointment.

We all need a champion's heart, as this experience is truly unforgettable. With this mindset, we can challenge ourselves to step out of our comfort zones and dare to do something different, honoring ourselves and our team members as well. That is the addiction of victories; once you taste it, you will never forget it.

A big applause to all of you! Mahnoor Ali Cheema, Sara Asghari, Chirag Ashiyani, Mattias Jakobsson, Roman Dolanski

data.commentary:
New Year, New Project #7

Grafana + Splunk on Azure ARO (Practical Enterprise Integration)

Many teams using Splunk today ask the same question: "Can we introduce Grafana without disrupting what already works?" The short answer is yes — if you design it as an integration strategy, not a tool replacement.

What we are implementing:
- GitOps-first delivery with ArgoCD
- Grafana on Azure ARO for operational dashboards and platform visibility
- Splunk retained for log intelligence and analysis
- Continuous feedback loop from Splunk findings to logging configuration tuning

Why this matters:
- Better signal quality
- Lower noise and unnecessary ingestion
- Faster troubleshooting for engineering teams
- Clearer ownership between platform and application layers

Observability is powerful. But unchecked, it becomes expensive noise. Here's the architecture we built to balance reliability, visibility, and cost:
→ Delivery — Teams push config to GitOps. ArgoCD syncs. ARO runs.
→ Observability — SignalFx feeds metrics into Grafana. Dashboards stay sharp.
→ Insight — App logs flow to Splunk. Patterns surface automatically.
→ The loop — Splunk insights feed back into logging config via GitOps. Less noise. Lower cost. Better signal.

The key insight: the feedback loop is the product. Without it, you're just paying more for more dashboards.

Built with: Azure Red Hat OpenShift · Grafana · SignalFx · Splunk · ArgoCD

This is not about "more monitoring tools." It is about building a monitoring model that is scalable, cost-aware, and enterprise-ready. If your organization is Splunk-first and considering Grafana, this path is realistic and incremental. 

#Grafana #Splunk #Azure #ARO #ArgoCD #GitOps #Observability #PlatformEngineering #SRE #CloudArchitecture

data.commentary:
12 Github repositories every AI Engineer should know! 
(Covering ML, RAG, PyTorch, MLOps, Agents)
Bookmark them! 👇 

1️⃣ Machine Learning for Beginners by Microsoft
→ Start here if you’re brand new to ML.
Covers basic ML concepts in Jupyter notebooks beginner-friendly and visual.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/gGithBVP

2️⃣ Learn PyTorch for Deep Learning
→ A great repo to learn PyTorch - could be a bit outdated but the concepts still applies.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/d4ptSuUY

3️⃣ Hands-on Large Language Models
→ This repo supports the Hands-On LLM book.
Learn everything from basic language models to finetuning with real notebooks.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/gpvPemeG

4️⃣ AI Agents for Beginners
→ A fantastic beginner-friendly course to get started with AI agents.
Free 11-lesson hands-on curriculum - no fluff, just code.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/gVm5gmv7

5️⃣ Prompt Engineering Guide
→ One-stop-shop for prompt engineering.
Guides, papers, lectures, and tons of curated examples.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/gDt3Sknr

6️⃣ LLM Course
→ Hands-on course covering the entire LLM lifecycle — design to deployment.
Includes roadmaps + Colab notebooks.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/gUPJmWNM

6️⃣ GenAI Agents
→ Great tutorials + code for building agent-based LLM systems.
Covers everything from simple tool-using agents to advanced workflows.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/gaiZgzpA

7️⃣ RAG Techniques
→ One of the most comprehensive and dynamic collections of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) tutorials available
🔗 https://lnkd.in/dcGtjFgY

8️⃣ Agents towards production
→ It is your hands-on guide to every building block of a GenAI agent stack.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/dnnkrJbE

9️⃣ Awesome Generative AI
→ Massive curated list of GenAI tools, papers, interviews, repos, and more. Use it as your personal R&D knowledge base.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/gsTk5nXx

🔟 Made With ML
→ Covers full ML product lifecycle: from design to CI/CD and monitoring. If you’re serious about building production-grade ML systems, this is gold.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/gn2RhT_m

1️⃣1️⃣ Designing Machine Learning Systems
→ Summaries + code + diagrams from the popular O’Reilly book.
A must-read if you want to architect real-world ML pipelines.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/g26KNXfb

1️⃣2️⃣ Jam With AI
→ Hands on RAG and AI Agent projects
🔗 https://lnkd.in/dkphZt25

♻️ Repost & save if you found it insightful 💚
➕ Join 48.000+ AI/ML builders here: https://lnkd.in/ds_SzEUH

A bird perches on a branch not because it trusts the branch, but because it trusts its own wings. This message of resilience and self-reliance resonated at today’s inspiring Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) Gothenburg meetup event at #Kognic, where innovation took center stage. The innovations from Kognic and #Swamp were truly inspiring. It’s all about showing up with skill, curiosity, and real momentum—supporting bold visions by learning, adapting, and acting. Growth isn’t about waiting for permission; it’s about learning, adapting, and taking action.

In this era of AI and equal rights, a well-designed strategy can set a company apart from the competition. The more you concentrate on your AI strategy, the more you will distinguish yourself in future competitions.

Hats off to everyone advancing with purpose.

article.title: Fair Transition — Transition Clause for Swedish Citizenship
article.subtitle: fairtransitionsweden.com

actor.name: Fair Transition
actor.description: 861 followers

data.commentary:
If you submit an application to a Swedish authority, follow every rule, pay every fee, and wait in the queue assigned you, should the rules be allowed to change while you wait?

Lagrådet said no. The government's own inquiry said no. 379 engineers and researchers at companies including Spotify, Klarna, Volvo, Ericsson, and Nvidia said no in their formal remissvar.

The reform itself can move forward on June 6. That isn't what we're contesting. We're asking for one thing: that the roughly 100,000 applications already sitting at Migrationsverket be assessed under the rules that existed when they were filed.

That's what transitional provisions are for. Every Nordic country uses them. Sweden has used them before. The institutions Sweden built to advise on exactly this kind of question recommended them here too.

fairtransitionsweden.com

data.commentary:
Attended the “Microsoft Agentic AI Hack” today at World of Volvo and finished the task first. 

What I learned is that regardless of how advanced the agent frameworks become, infrastructure remains crucial for the success of your project. Much of the time was spent on typical developer tasks such as:

- SSL/proxy issues
- Python version conflicts
- Data seeding
- Azure login/logout loops
- Agent registration mismatches
- Installing uv/Aspire/truststores

Essentially, everything except “AI.” I didn’t have enough time to fully grasp what each agent was designed to solve. Personally, I still prefer running things on-prem on my Mac. Once you commit to one ecosystem (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google), you lose the flexibility to choose.

Currently, I’m running Claude Code with Ollama Qwen 3.6 locally on an M5 Max MacBook Pro. I replicated the entire hackathon setup at home in about 30 minutes—clean and simple.

AI is evolving rapidly, but the fundamentals remain the same: computing, networking, environment setup, and debugging. If you can’t manage those, the agents won’t be of much help. 

#AgenticAI #MicrosoftAI #AIHackathon #DeveloperLife #InfrastructureMatters #LocalLLM #Ollama #Qwen #ClaudeCode #MacBookPro

actor.name: Patrick Henry Gallen
actor.description: Futures // Migration Justice // Europe for Everyone

data.commentary:
Efter över ett års kamp – först som individ och sedan som talesperson för Fair Transition – går Tidöregeringens proposition om skärpta krav för svenskt medborgarskap till omröstning i riksdagen på onsdag den 29 april. Att lagen kommer att röstas igenom är i princip helt säkert. Hur den kommer att se ut är det inte.

Tack vare tusentals engagerade och hårt arbetande människor runt om i landet finns fortfarande en möjlighet att påverka utfallet. Om bara ett fåtal ledamöter från Liberalerna väljer att stå upp för den svenska rättsstaten och en rättvis migrationspolitik tillsammans med oppositionen, kan övergångsbestämmelser säkras. Då skulle de nya reglerna endast gälla ansökningar som lämnas in efter den 6 juni.

Utan sådana övergångsregler – som både regeringens egna utredare och Lagrådet har rekommenderat – riskerar 100 000 människor att få avslag på deras ansökningar. Människor som i många fall har väntat i över fyra år på besked från Migrationsverket (Swedish Migration Agency).

Sverige riskerar dessutom att bli det ENDA landet i Europa som inför skärpta medborgarskapskrav utan övergångsbestämmelser. Det underminerar rättsstaten, urholkar tilliten till våra institutioner och skadar vår förmåga att attrahera och behålla den internationella kompetens som vår ekonomi är beroende av.

Jag påverkas inte själv av de nya reglerna. Jag hade turen att bli svensk medborgare i oktober – den viktigaste händelsen i mitt liv. Men jag har inte glömt den sårbarhet, den förlamande ångest och den totala maktlöshet jag kände som icke-EU-medborgare varje dag innan dess. Och jag kommer aldrig att glömma den livsavgörande lättnad och trygghet som medborgarskapet innebar.

När jag fick mitt medborgarskap lovade jag mig själv att aldrig glömma – och att aldrig sluta kämpa för dem som är mest politiskt och juridiskt utsatta. Jag kommer alltid att använda de rättigheter och privilegier som det lilla röda passet ger för att verka för ett mer rättvist samhälle.

Om man blir svensk medborgare innan den 6 juni ska inte vara en fråga om tur - och därför måste den nya lagen införas med rimliga övergångsbestämmelser.

Nu har våra politiker i riksdagen 48 timmar kvar att avgöra om de står upp för rättsstaten – och för ett Sverige byggt på tillit och öppenhet, snarare än rädsla, kortsiktighet och symbolpolitik.

Jag hoppas att de fattar rätt beslut.
The Local Europe Becky Waterton Emma Löfgren Cecilia Rönn Malin Danielsson Niels Paarup-Petersen

data.commentary:
Never waste a good excuse to play with new tech.
This weekend’s “productivity tool” (that absolutely does not improve my productivity): the Starlink Mini.

My quick at‑home measurements:

• Link speed: 242 MB/s
• Download: 105 MB/s
• Upload: 23.4MB/s


While these speeds won’t replace traditional Wi‑Fi or 5G, the real value lies elsewhere: reliable internet access in locations with zero cellular coverage. For anyone working in remote environments—field research, outdoor production, emergency response, or simply “work from anywhere”—this is a meaningful shift.

I’m planning to test it in more challenging environments soon.
If someone has an RV（Husbil）to lend, I’m more than happy to take this experiment on the road.

If you’re curious about Starlink, use this link to start your journey and we could all get one month free of using
👉 https://lnkd.in/dPtGuBpT

And if you’re in Gothenburg, you’re welcome to stop by and see it in action.

#Starlink #Connectivity #RemoteWork #TechLife #SatelliteInternet #WorkFromAnywhere #Gothenburg

data.commentary:
The comparison process promises to be fascinating! While unstructured data is being "organized" by LLMs, do they actually perform better with faster response times in RAG scenarios?  

We’re taking inspiration from Andrej Karpathy and putting it to the test—let’s see if the way we construct our RAG systems really makes a difference and sense.

The performance is not just about speed. What if we could “enrich” the knowledge base itself by having LLMs generate novel, context-aware answers?  

Imagine this: instead of just retrieving facts, we’re “creating”insights—like smashing together breads, noodles, and pastas into a creative new dish, tailored exactly to the question at hand. That’s the future of RAG: not just retrieval, but intelligent synthesis.

Resource：https://lnkd.in/eSzCYjZ2

#RAG #LLM #LargeLanguageModels #AIResearch #ArtificialIntelligence #MachineLearning #DataScience #Innovation

actor.name: Harvad Li
actor.description: Full-stack Developer @ Volvo Group | Cloud-Native (Kubernetes, Microservices) | Cloud Architect | DevOps | FinOps

data.commentary:
From the dust on the laptops, you can tell which one gets the most love.

The first one? My #PinebookPro. Last opened nearly half a year ago. The angry emojis? That’s from when I helped my daughter review math exam questions — let’s just say she wasn’t impressed. 🙃  
Truth is, without solid app support and ecosystem maturity, it’s been gathering dust more than doing real work. Now I've installed Kali on it — it's almost the best Linux OS for this device.

The second: a #LenovoThinkpadE15 Gen 3 running Windows 11. I open it maybe once a month — mostly to test a project on another platform or upload something to Google Drive. It does its job, but it’s not my go-to. 

And then there’s the third: my #MacminiM4Pro — 64GB RAM, 2TB SSD. Running 24/7 as my #homelab. No dust. No breaks. This is the one I actually live on.

So — what about you?  
Which machine at home do you actually *use most*, and why?  
Let’s swap setups and stories.

Happy weekend, everyone!

#MacMini #M4Pro #PinebookPro #HomeLab #AppleSilicon #LinuxEcosystem #DevSetup #LenovoE15 #ARMComputing #HomeOffice
